title: [lora_l2.c:713] lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64 returns alias with no thread safety
description: Function: lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64. Contract violation: Returns a pointer to internal state (lora_state.eui64) without mutex protection. The header documents this (line 176: 'The returned pointer aliases internal state. Do NOT modify. Contents are stable after init() completes.'). However, if deinit() is called while a caller holds the pointer, the eui64 buffer could be reused. Since eui64 is not zeroed in deinit() and init() only writes it once (idempotent), this is safe in practice. Priority 3 - design note for future maintainers.
close_reason: Fixed: Added thread safety documentation to lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() in both header (lora_l2.h) and implementation (lora_l2.c). The function returns an alias to internal state; callers must ensure no concurrent deinit() while using the pointer, or copy the value immediately.
